title: Upholstered Wood Storage Stool
summary: A rolling storage box topped with a velcro-attached upholstered cushion — a plywood-and-1x12 cube with foam and fabric on top, so the storage compartment stays fully accessible.
description:
This stool is a genuinely simple plywood box with one deliberate twist: the padded top isn't hinged or fixed, it's held on with industrial-strength velcro, so the entire storage compartment underneath opens by just lifting the cushion off. Build the box first with pocket holes, then attach the bottom with nails or staples and glue — a straightforward small carcass with nothing unusual in its joinery.

Caster wheels go on the bottom next, turning what would be a static ottoman into something that rolls — genuinely useful for a stool that's also functioning as hidden storage in a room where furniture gets shuffled around. **Two locking casters are worth using if this doubles as a step stool**, since an ottoman that rolls out from under a foot mid-step is a real fall risk.

The top is upholstered separately from the box: foam over a 3/4" plywood panel, wrapped in fabric and stapled underneath, then attached to the top of the box with velcro rather than hinges or screws. This is what keeps the storage genuinely convenient — no hinge hardware to bind or wear out, just lift the cushion straight up.

materials.name: 3/4" project panel, 18 in x 18 in (top and bottom)
Foam, 2 in thick, 16 in x 16 in
Upholstery fabric, 24 in x 24 in minimum, batting recommended if thin
Screws, 3/4", to fit casters if not supplied

cutList.material: 3/4" project panel, 18 in x 18 in (top and bottom)

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two box sides at 16-1/2" and two box front/back at 18" from the 1x12 project panel. Cut two box top and bottom at 18" from the 3/4" project panel, 18 in x 18 in (top and bottom). Cut one upholstered top panel at 16" from the 3/4" plywood scrap, 16 in x 16 in. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.
materials: 3/4" project panel, 18 in x 18 in (top and bottom)

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the box sides and front/back into a square frame using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the bottom panel with nails or staples and wood glue.
materials: 3/4" project panel, 18 in x 18 in (top and bottom)

steps.title: Attach caster wheels
steps.body: Attach caster wheels to the bottom of the box, using at least two locking casters if the stool will double as a step stool.
materials: Screws, 3/4", to fit casters if not supplied

steps.title: Upholster the top
steps.body: Wrap the foam and fabric around the 3/4" plywood top panel, stapling the fabric underneath. Attach the upholstered top panel to the box top with industrial strength velcro.
materials: Foam, 2 in thick, 16 in x 16 in
Upholstery fabric, 24 in x 24 in minimum, batting recommended if thin

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand any exposed wood surfaces before upholstering, and finish the box exterior as desired.

images.alt: Rolling storage stool with an upholstered lift-off cushion top
